original_image_tags: Hand-drawn map and trajectory diagram on aged paper. The map depicts a coastal area, likely in New England, showing islands labeled "Puffin I.", "Bear I.", "Egg Rock", "Duck I.", "Little Duck I.", "Englishman Bay", and "Bass Harbor Head Light". A trajectory line with arrows indicates an object's path from right to left (East to West). A circle is drawn around "St. Paul Light". A smaller diagram in the lower left shows altitude vs. distance. A compass rose points North to the right. A point is marked "Gerhune residence - Keduck".

elements.description: Hand-drawn map depicting a coastline with islands labeled 'Graham Is.', 'Maud I.', and 'Berry I.' A dark line representing a trajectory runs across the map with annotations including 'path of flight - approx 2 miles', 'possible error of course', and 'flame went out here'. A cross-shaped symbol represents an airfield labeled 'Sparwood B.C.'. Handwritten data lists details such as 'Duration of flight - app 10 seconds', 'Speed of object - 1000 to 1200 m.p.h.', and 'Time and date - April 8-49 4:15 PM'. A small graph at the bottom plots 'Trajectory' against altitude markers (500' to 2500').

judge.reason: Descriptions A and B are entirely about different visual content: A describes a New England coastal map with specific island names and a trajectory from East to West, while B describes a different map of a flight path over Sparwood B.C. with different labels, dates, and annotations, indicating they are analyzing two completely different images.
